sections.paragraph:
to live softly and delicately, not used in Kal. (Arab. غَنجَ to allure, to entice, used of the amorous gestures of women, in their looks, walk, etc.)

Pual, part. fem. to be soft and delicate, Jer 6:2.

Hithpael-

(1) i.q. Pual, Deu 28:56, compare Isa 55:2.

(2) to delight oneself, to be glad in any thing, followed by עַל Job 22:26 27:10 Psa 37:11 followed by מִן Isa 66:11.

(3) Hence used in a bad sense, to deride any one, followed by עַל Isa 57:4.

Derived nouns, תַּעֲנוּג and-
